title: otolepis cordigera
intro: Otolepis cordigera, also known as "tree nettle" or " Stinging tree nettle", is a plant species in the family Urticaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Otolepis cordigera include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of Otolepis cordigera include teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution due to the presence of urtic acid and other irritant compounds.
